item.dc:creator: StellaMatutina

item.title: KITTY!
item.description:
Y'all. It has been a DOOZY. Ms. Kitty, as all of you know is my inside bowling ball. She used to be outside but ever since the ear infection that caused her permanent head tilt she has been scared to go out. Sometimes she'll venture to the stoop if I have the door open but never further. Well I guess she was outside and I accidentally closed her out before the trash truck passed Thursday morning and she must have gotten spooked. Didn't notice she was missing until Thursday afternoon. Called her , searched for her, nothing. I did that multiple times a day and never saw or heard from her so by yesterday I had pretty much given up hope. I was outside with Darwin and I heard her meow, it's very specific. And I found her in the fence line of my backyard property!!!
She is so happy to be back inside. Last night she kept coming find me in bed and meowing for me to pet her. She couldn't contain her excitement
My girl!!! So glad she's safe.
item.dc:creator: vudumommajuju

item.title: A life-changing event.
item.description:
I don't have the source for this, just the video. My original upload cropped the video, so it wasn't complete. Here is the full thing. I appreciate your patience.
I'd like to hear if any of you have any similar experiences.
My wife has a few stories like this. She grew up in Kansas as one of two black children in the county. The nonchalant behavior of what is now the red hat groupIs... told... quite well here.
item.dc:creator: SquaresUnfiltered

item.title: 23 nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 238
item.description: “A person who has good thoughts cannot ever be ugly. You can have a wonky nose and a crooked mouth and a double chin and stick-out teeth, but if you have good thoughts they will shine out of your face...

item.title: Flock safety for pedophiles
item.description: Read the entire report here: https://jasonhunyar.substack.com/p/why-are-flock-employees-watching-720?r=7c08v8 I don't care if it's for technical reasons, product demos, or whatever excuse we'll hear. There is simply no acceptable reason to remotely view children via a preschool or private gym's cameras unless a serious crime was committed. Your local government might be discussing surveillance tech like Flock cameras, facial recognition, or automated license plate readers right now. This map helps you find those meetings and take action. https://alpr.watch/ source: @bennjordan in yt: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/YwVBsFD7v84 #flock #cameras #alpr #bennjordan #benn
item.dc:creator: djhash

item.title: Let's have a gay time
item.description:
Queer Mexicana by @KaiLysander
A reminder for why the L comes first in LGBTQ+ ? It’s not random — it’s history. Lesbians were on the front lines caring for gay men during the AIDS crisis, showing up when governments and systems didn’t. Putting the L first is about recognition, respect, and honouring that solidarity. ✨ Know the history. Respect the community. WE LOVE YOU LESBIANS
I know it's not pride month. I don't care. It's pride 24/7, 365
Credit: @peterparkerhasbigbongos
item.dc:creator: cryptidofthewood

item.title: Happening TODAY in Texas!
item.description: "call to arms" "virus in your community" Know the key words/phrases are being used to manipulate your views by these fascist corpo asshats. Their tactics have never changed. They will always use threats/fear/blame to persuade you to give your own freedom away. Then when you will protest about your loss, they will turn and say - "you wanted this, you voted for this" Don't let them.

item.title: Fuckin idiots the whole lot of them
item.description: Trump’s negotiators didn’t realize they agreed to allowing Iran to continue uranium enrichment in the non-English version of the agreement because Kash Patel and Marco Rubio fired all the Iranian experts last year who spoke Farsi to keep up with DOGE cuts. item.title: MAMDANI: “The wealth of a median white household in the city is more than $200,000, while that of a black household is less than $20,000 …
item.description: MAMDANI: “The wealth of a median white household in the city is more than $200,000, while that of a black household is less than $20,000 … We are reckoning with the long history of racism here and starting to act upon a framework that puts equity right at the center of it.”
item.dc:creator: jee22

item.title: Satisfying Dump about quality shoes. Have a better day !
item.description:
2 hours to get this customers shoes finished before closing.
Algumas coisas de qualidade podem ser reparadas. Quality things may be reparated.
Emerald green patina at its finest.
Italian Shoes.
Ultimate shoe shine.
Proper Leather Shoe Shine Experience.
Restoration of an old shoe.
2,000-year-old Roman children's shoes found in the ruins of Palmyra. The leather and craftsmanship are still visible.
Buttoning shoes in the past.
Speed hooks for shoelaces on work boots.
Lost art of Handmade Shoe making.
Process of making $600 Handmade shoes.
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot

item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: It's Thigh Time already in France
item.description:
This is a dress I found in the states at a thrift store
I love it cause it is super floral and I love the colors
AND IT HAS POCKETS
It's a cheap Lularoe brand
But very forgiving and flattering I think
It allows a lot of easy access for the wind to breeze underneath
The colors are really nice.
I know there are no thigh highs in these pics but I hope you still get some joy. Have a nice Thursday everyone.
item.dc:creator: princesscindycat

item.title: Ukrainian forces struck BOTH “Admiral Essen” and “Admiral Makarov” in Novorossiysk, these aren’t patrol boats, these are missile carriers
item.description: Each one worth hundreds of millions, capable of launching cruise missiles at Ukrainian cities, and now both sitting damaged or worse Hitting them in port makes it even worse for Russia, that’s supposed to be the SAFE zone This isn’t just damage, it’s capability being taken off the board, and that hits both the fleet AND strike capacity at the same time
item.dc:creator: vladrichdemaclant

item.title: I found this to be very informative!
item.description: In March 1963, a father woke up in the middle of the night to the sound of his five-year-old daughter crying. Her jaw was swollen. Her throat burned. She had the mumps. Most fathers would have comforted the child and hoped for the best. Maurice Hilleman drove to his laboratory in the dark, retrieved a sterile cotton swab, drove back home, gently wiped the back of his daughter Jeryl Lynn’s throat, and returned to the lab. He placed the swab in a vial of nutrient broth, labeled it with her name, and put it in the deep freezer. Then he began the work that would eventually save millions of children from diseases that once emptied classrooms and filled graveyards. He was born in 1919 on a remote farm near Miles City, Montana. His twin sister died during birth. His mother died two days later. Raised by his aunt and uncle in an isolated farmhouse heated by wood stoves, he learned early that nature was not kind. It was a ruthless biological machine that required dismantling. Diphtheria swept through local schools every winter. Children developed thick gray membranes in their throats and suffocated in their beds while the local doctor could offer only cold compresses, whiskey, and sympathy. Young Maurice raised chickens, cleaned coops, and watched how sickness moved through a flock. Fifty years later, those Montana chicken eggs would become the foundation of his greatest scientific breakthroughs. He earned a scholarship to the University of Chicago and studied microbiology. In 1957, while working at the Walter Reed Army Institute of Research, he read a short newspaper article about a severe influenza outbreak overwhelming clinics in Hong Kong. He immediately cabled a military medical officer in Japan and requested a saliva sample from an infected patient. When it arrived, he analyzed the strain and compared it against American blood serum. No one in the United States had antibodies. The entire population was vulnerable. He calculated shipping lanes and timelines, then told his superiors a pandemic would strike the American mainland by the first week of September. The military dismissed him. The World Health Organization called his timeline alarmist. Maurice ignored them. He bypassed bureaucracy entirely. He contacted the six largest pharmaceutical manufacturers in the country, sent them frozen virus samples, and told them to stop their regular production lines and begin manufacturing a new vaccine immediately. He also contacted poultry farmers across the nation and instructed them not to slaughter their roosters — the manufacturers would need millions of fertilized eggs to grow the virus. The companies took the financial risk without government approval. The Asian Flu arrived exactly when Maurice predicted. Thanks to his unauthorized push, forty million doses of vaccine were already shipping to hospitals. The virus killed 70,000 Americans. Public health experts estimate it would have killed one million without his intervention. He didn’t pause to celebrate. He moved on to the next threat. In March 1963, after swabbing Jeryl Lynn’s throat, he began the painstaking process of creating a live-attenuated mumps vaccine. He passed the virus through cultures of chick embryo cells, week after week, forcing it to adapt to chicken cells and become progressively weaker at infecting humans. He was not a gentle man. He ran his lab with military precision, swore at executives, and kept a row of replica shrunken heads on his desk as a warning to technicians who mislabeled samples. Fourteen-hour shifts were normal. Complaints were met with a calm reminder that viruses do not take weekends off. By the seventeenth passage, the virus was sufficiently weakened. He tested it in animals. It held. To prove it worked in humans, he drew up a dose and injected it into the arm of his younger daughter, Kirsten. She did not develop mumps. Her blood showed a strong immune response. The vaccine was licensed in 1967. The Jeryl Lynn strain he isolated from his daughter’s throat remains the basis for every mumps vaccine used in the MMR shot today. He didn’t stop there. He combined mumps with measles and rubella into a single injection. He developed the first effective hepatitis B vaccine by purifying blood plasma through a rigorous chemical process using pepsin, urea, and formaldehyde — a method many called reckless until clinical trials proved it safe and capable of preventing liver cancer. He created vaccines for chickenpox and meningitis. In total, he developed more than forty vaccines. Eight of the fourteen routine childhood vaccines given today trace directly back to his laboratory. He retired in 1984 under a corporate mandatory retirement policy. He spent his later years advising global health organizations but rarely gave interviews and never sought the spotlight. He held no patents — the corporations did. He drew a standard laboratory salary his entire career. He died in 2005 at age 85 and is buried in a simple plot in a Pennsylvania cemetery. The grass around his stone is cut every other Tuesday. The global medical consensus estimates that Maurice Hilleman’s work saves approximately eight million lives every single year. The diseases that once terrorized children in the 1920s — mumps, measles, rubella, hepatitis B — are now historical footnotes in much of the world. He never put his own name on the vial. He simply put the cure inside it. This is the story of the most important scientist most people have never heard of: Maurice Hilleman, the man who erased the diseases of childhood. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maurice_Hilleman
item.dc:creator: Ice2Ice2

item.title: Peak Masculinity
item.description: Canadian Astronaut Jeremy Hansen and US Astronaut Reid Wiseman embracing and crying after Hansen called down to name a previously undiscovered crater Carroll who had recently died of cancer. The crew had previously discussed this as a possibility. This is what real men do for each other, they support each other and love each other.
